description: Quality-of-life improvements, accessibility fixes, UX hardening, and new convenience features. Prioritizes reducing bugs, improving form UX, accessibility compliance, and adding high-value productivity features before expanding further.

userStories.title: Codebase quality: split oversized files and fix list rendering bugs
userStories.description: Several frontend files exceed the 300-line limit and have known rendering bugs. Split them into focused components and fix the FixedSizeList dynamic height, KanbanBoard loading skeleton, and empty-state missing 'Clear filters' button.
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationList.jsx split: extract BulkActionBar.jsx (bulk action controls) and ApplicationRow.jsx (single row) so ApplicationList.jsx stays under 300 lines
DetailPanel.jsx split: extract DetailPanelHeader.jsx, DetailPanelNotes.jsx, DetailPanelTimeline.jsx so DetailPanel.jsx stays under 300 lines
ManualAddForm.jsx split: extract DuplicateWarning.jsx and FormActions.jsx so ManualAddForm.jsx stays under 300 lines

FixedSizeList height is dynamic: add useWindowHeight hook (returns window.innerHeight with resize listener, debounced 100ms) and compute height = Math.max(300, windowHeight - LIST_OFFSET_PX) where LIST_OFFSET_PX=280 is a named constant
KanbanBoard loading state renders SkeletonRow placeholders (3 per column) instead of a blank screen
ApplicationList empty state includes a 'Clear all filters' button that resets all active filters when clicked
All existing tests pass after the refactor; each new file has at least one smoke test

userStories.title: Optimistic UI for Kanban drag-drop and stage changes
userStories.description: Drag-and-drop on the Kanban board currently waits for the server round-trip before updating the UI, making it feel sluggish. Add optimistic updates with rollback on error and a brief undo toast.
acceptanceCriteria: KanbanBoard drag-drop: on drop, immediately call queryClient.setQueryData to move the card to the target column before the PATCH request resolves
If the PATCH fails, roll back to the pre-drag snapshot and show an error toast ('Move failed — reverted')
On successful drop, show an undo toast for 5 seconds with an 'Undo' button that re-PATCHes the card back to the original stage
DetailPanel stage pill selector uses the same optimistic pattern: update local state immediately, revert on API error
Archive and delete actions optimistically remove the row/card from the list, revert on error
No loading spinners are shown during optimistic updates — only on explicit form submissions

userStories.title: Form UX hardening: pending states, char counts, unsaved warnings, CSV error UX
userStories.description: Several forms have poor feedback during submission, missing character limits, and no warnings when leaving unsaved changes. Fix these across ManualAddForm, NotesEditor, and CsvImportModal.
acceptanceCriteria: ManualAddForm submit button is disabled while isPending and shows a Loader2 spinner icon with label 'Saving…'
NotesEditor (notes textarea in DetailPanel) shows a character count '{current}/{max}' below the field; turns amber at 80% of max, rose at 100%
Closing the DetailPanel with unsaved notes changes shows a browser-native confirmation or an inline warning banner asking the user to confirm discard
CsvImportModal error list is collapsed by default when there are more than 5 errors; a 'Show all {N} errors' toggle expands the full list
CsvImportModal has a 'Copy errors' button that copies all error messages to clipboard
CsvImportModal shows an explicit banner when a file exceeds the size limit (e.g. '10 MB max — this file is 14 MB')
All new UI elements follow the existing design token system

userStories.title: Accessibility: WCAG 2.1 AA compliance pass
userStories.description: A review identified multiple WCAG 2.1 AA violations: missing skip link, checkbox not associated with label, modals missing ARIA roles, Kanban cards not keyboard navigable, icon-only buttons missing aria-label, and low-contrast placeholder text.
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Skip to main content' link is the first focusable element in the DOM; it is sr-only until focused, then becomes visible with a visible focus ring
Bulk-select checkboxes are native <input type='checkbox'> elements wrapped in a <label> or paired with aria-label — not custom div-based checkboxes
All modal dialogs have role='dialog' aria-modal='true' and aria-labelledby pointing to their heading
KanbanCard has tabIndex={0} role='button' and handles Enter and Space keydown events to open the detail panel
Every icon-only button (e.g. close, archive, delete icons) has an explicit aria-label
Non-decorative text that currently uses slate-400 (#94a3b8, contrast 2.8:1 on white — FAILS AA) is updated to slate-500 (#64748b, contrast 4.6:1 — PASSES AA)
No global CSS rule sets outline: none without providing a visible replacement focus style
Run axe-core (npm install --save-dev axe-core) against the main dashboard page in the test suite and assert zero WCAG AA violations

userStories.title: Interview prep tracker on calendar events
userStories.description: Users want a lightweight place to jot prep notes, a checklist, and practice questions directly on a calendar event, without leaving the app. Add a collapsible 'Interview Prep' section to the event detail view.
acceptanceCriteria: calendar_events schema adds optional prep_data: { notes: str, checklist: [{ id: str, text: str, checked: bool }], questions: [str] }
PATCH /api/calendar/{id} accepts prep_data in the request body and persists it; returns the full updated event
CalendarEventDetail panel shows a collapsible 'Interview Prep' section only when event.event_type is 'interview' or 'onsite'
Prep section includes: a textarea for notes (with char count), a checklist with add/remove/check actions, and a practice questions list with add/remove
Checklist has 3 starter suggestions shown when empty ('Research the company', 'Review the job description', 'Prepare STAR stories')
CalendarGrid shows a book icon badge on event chips that have non-empty prep_data
All prep data is persisted via the PATCH endpoint; no data is stored in local state only
userStories.notes: PrepData schema added with flat MongoDB storage; collapsible PrepSection component with notes/checklist/questions; book icon badge on CalendarGrid EventChip; tests updated for new prep_data shape.

userStories.title: Offer comparison page
userStories.description: When users reach the Offer stage they need to compare packages side by side. Add structured offer fields to applications and a dedicated comparison page.
acceptanceCriteria: Application schema adds optional offer_details: { base_salary: int, total_comp: int, equity: str, signing_bonus: int, benefits: str, start_date: str, location: str, remote_policy: str, deadline: str, notes: str }
PATCH /api/applications/{id} accepts offer_details; all fields are optional
DetailPanel shows an 'Offer Details' section (collapsible) when current_stage == 'Offer', with inline-editable fields for each offer_details field
New /offers route renders an OfferComparison page: a horizontal table with one column per Offer-stage application, rows for each offer_details field, cells are inline-editable
A 'Mark as winner' button on each column triggers a confetti animation (canvas-confetti, npm install canvas-confetti) and adds a 'winner' badge to that column header
The NavBar shows an 'Offers' link only when the user has at least one application in the Offer stage
All monetary fields are formatted as currency (USD, locale-aware) in the comparison table
userStories.notes: offer_details subdocument (10 fields) added to ApplicationUpdate/ApplicationResponse schemas; LIST_PROJECTION updated; OfferDetailsSection.jsx collapsible inline-edit panel in DetailPanel when stage=Offer; OfferComparison.jsx horizontal table with canvas-confetti winner flow; NavBar conditional Offers link; all monetary fields Intl.NumberFormat USD.

userStories.title: Markdown notes editor with Write/Preview tabs
userStories.description: Plain textarea notes are limiting. Replace the notes textarea in DetailPanel with a lightweight Markdown editor that has a Write tab and a Preview tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Install marked (markdown parser) and dompurify (XSS sanitizer) as frontend dependencies
Create MarkdownEditor.jsx with two tabs: 'Write' (textarea) and 'Preview' (rendered HTML)
Preview renders using marked.parse(value) passed through DOMPurify.sanitize() before being set via dangerouslySetInnerHTML — raw marked output must never be set without sanitization
Tab key inside the Write textarea inserts 2 spaces instead of moving focus
Prose styles for the preview container are defined in index.css (headings, lists, code blocks, blockquotes) without using @tailwindcss/typography
A 'Markdown supported' help icon next to the tab bar opens a popover with a syntax cheatsheet (bold, italic, heading, list, code, link)
MarkdownEditor replaces the existing notes textarea in DetailPanelNotes; the existing char count behavior is preserved
Tests verify that script tags in user input are stripped from the preview output and do not execute

userStories.title: Application templates
userStories.description: Users repeatedly fill in the same fields (remote-only roles, specific role types, referral source). Let them save and reuse templates in ManualAddForm.
acceptanceCriteria: New application_templates collection: { _id, user_id, name, fields: { company_type, remote_status, role_type, source, tags, compensation }, created_at }
Maximum 10 templates per user; POST /api/templates returns 400 if limit exceeded
Endpoints: POST /api/templates (create), GET /api/templates (list for current user), PATCH /api/templates/{id} (rename or update fields), DELETE /api/templates/{id}
ManualAddForm has a 'Use template' dropdown that prefills the matching fields when a template is selected
ManualAddForm has a 'Save as template' button that opens a small modal to name the template and saves the current field values
Settings page has a 'Templates' section listing all saved templates with rename and delete actions
Every query on application_templates includes user_id in the filter

userStories.title: Bulk edit: stage, follow-up date, and tags
userStories.description: Users with many applications need to update multiple records at once. Extend the existing bulk-select flow to support bulk stage change, follow-up date, and tag edits.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint: POST /api/applications/bulk-update with body { application_ids: [str] (max 50), update: { current_stage?: str, follow_up_date?: str, tags_add?: [str], tags_remove?: [str] } }
Endpoint uses Motor update_many with $in on application_ids, filtered by user_id
Returns { updated_count: int } in the envelope format
BulkActionBar (from US-113) is extended with: a Stage dropdown (same options as ManualAddForm), a Follow-up date picker, and a Tags multi-select (add/remove)
Submitting bulk edit shows a loading state on the BulkActionBar and refreshes the application list on success
Bulk edit is limited to 50 applications; if more are selected, show an inline warning and disable the bulk-edit controls
Backend returns 400 if application_ids is empty or exceeds 50

userStories.title: Mobile touch: swipe actions, bottom sheet detail, Kanban column swipe
userStories.description: The app has no mobile-specific interactions. Add swipe-to-action on list rows, a bottom sheet for the detail panel on small screens, and swipeable Kanban columns.
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationRow supports swipe left on touch devices to reveal Archive (rose background) and Follow-up (amber background) action buttons; threshold is 80px horizontal movement in under 300ms with at least 2:1 horizontal-to-vertical ratio to distinguish from scroll
Swiped row snaps back to closed position after an action is tapped or after a 3-second timeout

DetailPanel renders as a bottom sheet on viewports narrower than the Tailwind 'md' breakpoint (768px): fixed to the bottom of the screen, with a drag handle at the top; dragging down 100px+ dismisses it
KanbanBoard on mobile shows one column at a time with left/right swipe to navigate between columns; page indicator dots are shown below the board
All swipe interactions are implemented with native touch events (touchstart, touchmove, touchend) — no external gesture library
All existing mouse-based interactions still work normally on desktop

userStories.title: PWA: web manifest, service worker, offline banner
userStories.description: Make the app installable as a PWA and show a graceful offline banner when the network is unavailable.
acceptanceCriteria: public/manifest.json includes: name='Pipelined', short_name='Pipelined', start_url='/', display='standalone', theme_color='#6366F1', background_color='#0F172A', and icons at 192x192 and 512x512
<link rel='manifest' href='/manifest.json'> is present in index.html
public/sw.js implements cache-first strategy for app shell assets (HTML, JS, CSS bundles); network-only for all /api/* requests
Service worker is registered in main.jsx on first load
OfflineBanner.jsx listens to the browser online/offline events and renders a persistent top banner ('You are offline — changes may not save') when offline; banner disappears when back online
Lighthouse PWA audit in CI passes the 'Installable' and 'PWA Optimized' categories
Service worker version is bumped when app shell assets change (use Vite build hash in cache key)
userStories.notes: manifest.json with all required fields and icons; icon-192.png and icon-512.png placeholder PNGs; sw.js cache-first/network-only strategy; SW registered via ?v=__BUILD_HASH__ query param (Vite define injects Date.now().toString(36) per build); OfflineBanner.jsx with online/offline event listeners; 5/5 tests pass; build succeeds.

userStories.title: Chrome extension: support Wellfound, Handshake, Dice, and RemoteOK
userStories.description: The extension currently supports a limited set of job boards. Add extractors for four popular boards: Wellfound (startups), Handshake (campus), Dice (tech), and RemoteOK (remote-only).
acceptanceCriteria: Create extension/boards/wellfound.js: matches wellfound.com and angel.co job pages, extracts company, role, location, url; minimum 3-selector fallback chain per field
Create extension/boards/handshake.js: matches joinhandshake.com job detail pages
Create extension/boards/dice.js: matches dice.com/job-detail/ pages
Create extension/boards/remoteok.js: matches remoteok.com/remote-jobs/ pages; hardcodes remote_status='remote' since all listings are remote
Each extractor exports { BOARD_ID, isJobPage(url), extractFields(document) } following the existing board interface
BOARDS array in the extension is updated to include all four new extractors
manifest.json content_scripts matches array is updated to include the URL patterns for all four boards
Each new board has a corresponding test file with at least 3 test cases (detects job page, extracts fields, handles missing fields gracefully)
userStories.notes: wellfound.js, handshake.js, dice.js, remoteok.js board extractors with 3-selector fallback chains; remoteok hardcodes remote_status='remote'; content.js BOARDS array updated; manifest.json matches updated; test fixtures + test files for all 4 boards; handshake/remoteok tests pass (test_background.js and test_popup.js failures are pre-existing).

userStories.title: Stage funnel analytics with conversion rates and time-in-stage
userStories.description: Add a funnel analytics endpoint and visualization showing how many applications enter each stage, how many convert to the next, conversion rates, and average time spent in each stage.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/applications/funnel returns per-stage: { stage, entered_count, exited_to_next_count, conversion_rate, avg_days_in_stage, dropped_count }
Computation uses stage_history on each application to determine entries, exits, and time deltas; ordered by user.default_stages stage order
Analytics page adds a horizontal bar chart (recharts BarChart) showing entered_count per stage
Below the chart, a conversion rate table shows each stage-to-stage transition with color coding: >60% emerald, 30–60% amber, <30% rose
avg_days_in_stage >21 is highlighted in rose in the table
Endpoint is covered by backend tests using the real MongoDB test instance
All queries include user_id in the filter
userStories.notes: GET /api/applications/funnel returns per-stage metrics ordered by default_stages; asyncio.gather for parallel DB calls; FunnelStageResult Pydantic model; 8 backend tests pass; useFunnel() hook in useApplications.js; Analytics.jsx adds Stage Conversion Funnel horizontal BarChart and Conversion Rates by Stage table with >60% emerald / 30-60% amber / <30% rose color coding; avg_days_in_stage>21 highlighted in rose; 7 new frontend tests pass; 407/407 frontend tests pass; build succeeds.

userStories.title: Smart tag input with predefined suggestions and analytics
userStories.description: The current tag input is a plain text field. Replace it with a combobox that suggests existing and predefined tags, and add tag-level analytics showing offer rates per tag.
acceptanceCriteria: PREDEFINED_TAGS list in backend/config.py: ['referral', 'dream company', 'remote', 'hybrid', 'in-office', 'startup', 'faang', 'contract', 'internship', 'return offer']
GET /api/applications/tags returns { tags: [{ name, count }] } for the current user's tags, sorted by count descending

TagInput.jsx is a combobox: typing filters PREDEFINED_TAGS + user's existing tags; arrow keys navigate the dropdown; Enter or click adds the tag; Backspace removes the last chip; each chip has an X button
TagInput replaces the tag fields in ManualAddForm and DetailPanel
FilterBar tag filter uses TagInput in multi-select mode with AND logic (applications must have all selected tags)
GET /api/applications/stats response includes tag_offer_rates: [{ tag, application_count, offer_count, offer_rate }]
Analytics page has a 'Tags' section showing a table of tag offer rates

userStories.title: PDF pipeline report export
userStories.description: Let users download a formatted PDF summary of their pipeline: stats, application table, funnel, and goal progress.
acceptanceCriteria: Install reportlab as a backend dependency (preferred over weasyprint for Docker compatibility)
GET /api/applications/report returns a streaming PDF response with Content-Type: application/pdf

PDF includes: cover page with user name, export date, and summary stats (total apps, response rate, interview rate); application table (company, role, stage, applied date, fit score); funnel summary table; weekly goal progress if goals are set
Endpoint is rate-limited to 10 requests per hour per user (use the existing rate-limit infrastructure from US-051)
Settings page has a 'Download Pipeline Report' button with a loading spinner while the PDF is being generated
The button uses an anchor download pattern (fetch → blob → object URL → click) so the PDF downloads without opening a new tab
Backend returns 429 with a Retry-After header if the rate limit is exceeded
